channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony has a poignant vibe this year
item.description: The Rock & Roll Hall Fame induction ceremony on Saturday night promises to be starry, jamming — and bittersweet. So many of the honorees this time have been lost.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States to election officials: Follow the law or face consequences
item.description: In at least five battleground states, officials have investigated, indicted and even jailed officials who tried to interfere with the vote count.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Rob Rogers has seen his rural corner of New England turn into a stronghold for Donald Trump in two consecutive elections. But this year he's feeling hopeful that Vice President Kamala Harris could reclaim a potentially decisive electoral vote for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Naturita residents react to arrest of Dan Bishop
item.description: Jamie Allred, assistant manager at Wild Gals Market, “I was only 14 at the time, and there was a lot of confusion in the community. There were different rumors saying that he had just up and left without saying anything.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Kawamoto, KREX 5

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Wisconsin US Senate candidates trade accusations of lying during testy debate
item.description: Democratic U.S. Sen. Tammy Baldwin and Republican challenger Eric Hovde have repeatedly accused each other of lying during an often testy and confrontational debate.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A failed mic leaves Donald Trump pacing the stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donald Trump paced his rally stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes Friday night in Detroit after his microphone cut out.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: “I’ll tell you what I love, I love cows but if we go with Kamala you won’t have any cows any more,” the former president responded to the child's question. “I don’t want to ruin this kid's day.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump earned money from government sources through Washington hotel, Democrats find
item.description: Over 11 months while former President Trump was in office, his Washington, D.C., hotel took in some $300,000 from sources inside and outside of government, including tens of thousands in payments from the U.S. Secret Service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Texas was about to execute Robert Roberson. Then a last-ditch tactic bought him more time
item.description: Robert Roberson, who had been set to be the first person in the U.S. to be put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome, had his execution delayed after a dramatic few hours of legal wrangling among three different Texas courts.
item.dc:creator: Juan A. Lozano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate Republicans raise questions after funds for disaster loans exhausted
item.description: A group of GOP senators are pressing the Small Business Administration (SBA) for answers after officials said its disaster loan program exhausted its funds earlier this week in the aftermath of a pair of major hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former MTV VJ Ananda Lewis reveals stage 4 breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The TV personality first revealed she was diagnosed with breast cancer years ago, but it has progressed and metastasized since.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chutkan unseals much-redacted Jack Smith evidence used to build Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: A judge on Friday unsealed nearly 1,900 pages of evidence special counsel Jack Smith assembled in building the election interference case against former President Trump, publicly posting the highly redacted trove.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rules Delphi composite sketches aren't admissible
item.description: UPDATE: During Friday's proceedings, Judge Fran Gull ruled that the sketches aren't admissible in court. The state filed a motion this week seeking to prohibit the use of the two widely circulated sketches from the early years of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Nevada Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.) and Republican Sam Brown duked it out during their first and only debate Thursday night, making their pitches to voters in one of the key battleground states on the 2024 map and a must-win state for Democratic Senate majority hopes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' with Trump clip during Harris interview on Fox News
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said Thursday that he made "a mistake” in not including a specific clip of former President Trump during his interview with Vice President Harris on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Abortion is on the ballot in 10 states. Here's where the measures stand
item.description: Voters in 10 states will decide this November whether to adopt constitutional amendments that could shape the future of abortion access in their states.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: Harris holds 1-point lead over Trump nationally: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris leads former President Trump by 1 percentage point nationally, according to an Emerson College survey released Fridy.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine State Police's Lewiston after-action report points out flaws in deadly shooting
item.description: The Maine State Police announced that an independent review of the Lewiston mass shooting after-action report was completed by the New England State Police Administrators Conference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama executes man who killed 5 and asked to be put to death
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — Alabama executed a man Thursday who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals and asked to be put to death. Derrick Dearman, 36, was pronounced dead at 6:14 p.m. Thursday at Holman prison in southern Alabama.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, KRON4

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WGN 9

item.title: Missing teen kayaker found clinging to kayak off Waikiki
item.description: "I think he was in total shock because he wasn't emotional at all. I was actually crying my guts off because he was okay.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, KLAS, CBS News
